claim: Sharing the chain RANDOMIZE->INVERT->TRUNCATE between Goodhart's Law and No-Cloning Theorem is statistically significant
evidence: RERUN with rebuilt depth2_matrix. R->I->T chain present in 148/246 = 60.2% of hubs. R->I confidence: {'LOW': 144, 'MEDIUM': 4}. I->T confidence: {'LOW': 147, 'MEDIUM': 11}. Null-model p(two hubs share chain) = 0.361954. After Bonferroni for 729 chains: p = 1.000000. Goodhart in depth2 R->I->T set: True. No-Cloning in depth2 R->I->T set: False. Chain found through targeted depth-3 probing only (depth3_probes table, unchanged). Sharing this chain remains unremarkable even with differentiated confidence profiles.

details.rebuild_note: depth2_matrix rebuilt from source evidence. Confidence profiles now differentiated but R->I->T chain prevalence remains high.

implications_for_other_papers: The R->I->T chain has a high depth-2 base rate even with the rebuilt matrix. After Bonferroni correction for 729 possible depth-3 chains, the Goodhart/No-Cloning shared chain remains statistically unremarkable. Cross-domain structural isomorphisms in Noesis v2 should be tested with similar null models.
